---
title: "Brain-to-Text '25: Decoding Speech from Neural Activity"
author:
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2025-09-11
date-format: long
image: "../../images/Brain-to-Text25.png"

categories:
  - Projects
  - ML Marathon
  - MLM25
  - Deep learning
  - Signal processing
  - Time-series
  - NLP
  - Neuroscience
  - RNN

---

Brain-to-Text '25 was featured in the [2025 Machine Learning Marathon (MLM25)](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/). This Kaggle competition challenges participants to decode intracortical neural activity during attempted speech into text -- aiming to restore communication for people with paralysis.

### Challenge design
- **Task**: Decode neural recordings from speech-related brain regions into the words a participant is attempting to say.
- **Domain**: Brain-computer interfaces and neural speech decoding.
- **Data**: A new intracortical speech neuroscience dataset provided for the competition.
- **Methods**: The 2024 edition's top approaches used RNN ensembles merged with fine-tuned large language models. The baseline achieved 9.7% word error rate; the top entrant reached 5.8%.

### Links
- **Kaggle challenge**: [Brain-to-Text '25](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/brain-to-text-25)

## Related resources
- [**Workshop**: Intro to Deep Learning with PyTorch](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-Deeplearning_PyTorch.html): Learn RNNs and sequence modeling fundamentals in PyTorch — key building blocks for neural speech decoding.
- [**Workshop**: Intro to Natural Language Processing (NLP)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-TextAnalysis_Python.html): Brush up on text processing and language model basics relevant to the text decoding target.
- [**Library**: Hugging Face](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Libraries/HuggingFace.html): Top solutions fine-tuned LLMs from Hugging Face — learn how to discover and use open-source models.

## Comments
